title: Justicia callothamnum
intro: Justicia calothamnus, also known as "Queen's Tears", is a medicinal plant species in the Acanthaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Justicia calothamnus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Justicia calothamnus is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Justicia callothamnum is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Justicia, which comprises over 600 species of flowering plants. The family Acanthaceae, to which Justicia callothamnum belongs, is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or acanthus family. This family is characterized by the presence of spines or bracts in their leaves. Justicia callothamnum's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Acanthaceae family.
